title: Does Ouroboros bite its tail in 30
display_title: Does Ouroboros bite its tail in 30
wikitext: []: [SPOILERS] Does Ouroboros bite its tail in 3.0?             Quote Post by Olmer » Sun Nov 18, 2012 10:02 am Hi, everyone. I'm one of those guys, who have been lurking here for quite some time and decided to start posting right now to share their views on 3.0. English is not my first language, so I'd like to apologize in advance for its quality ;) I haven't seen the movie yet, so a lot of theories I'm about to make are going to have weak fundaments. Hopefully, they will give you some food for thought and maybe you'll refine them. So, the general idea is that, as the characters age in EVA, they start filling the shoes of other characters, inheriting their traits, repeating their mistakes etc. People say, that 3.0 is totally different than anything that came before, but really - isn't it the same old story with new generations of people taking place of the oldies? Isn't Ouroboros biting its tail in 3.0? I may be going too far with this, but there are some similarities that I would like to point out. In general, there are 3 generations (not tied by age 100% accurately) (this is the state in NGE. In 3.0 it changes its dynamics): First generation represented by Keel and Yui Second generation represented by Gendo and Misato Third generation represented by Shinji and Asuka Each generation is composed of a man, who sets a (potentially selfish) goal for himself, is ready to sacrifice entire world for it and is universally hated for it (we can only assume things work like that for Keel, though) and a powerful woman who tries to stop him. Those generations may also be associated with the character's "rank" in the story. The lowest generation members are pawns/soldiers, the middle generation members are rebels (they don't literally have to rebel against something, but they have the mindset and tools to force their will on the world) and the higher generation members are leaders of the sides of a conflict (loosely speaking). As the generations members age, they start to show similarities to the representatives of older generations. The most obvious example is Shinji becoming Gendo (shifting from generation three to generation two). After 3.0 Shinji is basically a man, who is universally hated/misunderstood by anybody for trying to get back "his woman". He also took active part in starting TWO impacts, just like Gendo in NGE. I can honestly see those two finally reaching an understanding in 4.0. They're really similar at this point imho. Also Shinji advances from being a pawn into being a rebel by "betraying" WILL-E (showing signs of free will). Gendo becoming Keel is more shaky (everything involving Keel is shaky in this theory, as we don't know much about Keel at all). It's based on just two clues really: Gendo getting a vizier which really makes him resemble Keel visually and Gendo being (seemingly) in much better understanding with Keel in 3.0 than in EoE. I mean, Keel and Gendo survived 14 years together without killing each other, so...Gendo also advances from rebel into the main side of the conflict against Misato. Just like NGE may be viewed as a conflict between Yui's and Keel's divine plans, 3.0 seems to be a conflict between Misato's and Gendo's grand schemes. The part of the theory that deals with women of Evangelion, is more interesting. Misato in NGE is (trying to be) a mother figure for Shinji. She's also somewhere between the pawn and the rebel on the axis, as she tries to find the truth about Nerv, losing a bit of her faith in the honesty of the organization in the process. However, in 3.0 she's been given an awful lot of power. She basically has a warship powered by S2 engine, equipped with cannons that can supposedly pierce AT fields. She becomes a side in the conflict, reaching First Generation. People are shocked, that she's really cold towards Shinji in 3.0, as she loses her "motherly love" traits in favor of more "godlike" status fitting for someone like Yui. There is a dissonance between Yui remembered by Shinji, and Yui in EVA-01. Even though it is established that all she did, she did for Shinji, by the end of EoE her plan is arguably more important to her, than the well-being of her son (she waits for Asuka's death before activating). I can see Misato going through the same change from a mother/guardian into a god. She fills Yui's shoes. And she seems to have Yui's approval as I don't believe Yui would just silently become power source for Wunder instead of blowing it into pieces, if she didn't support Misato's intentions. And then there's Asuka. Now technicaly being 28, which is close to the age of NGE Misato. She's still "just" a pilot, but hardly a pawn anymore (rebel on the axis). She's also been given much more power than in NGE. Although the details about the final battle in 3.0 vary, we are safe to assume, that she holds her own against Shinji+Kaworu "almost beating them before she runs out of power" and then she still prevents Rei from damaging Wunder. She also shows some Misato-like behaviour: dragging depressed Shinji around after Kaworu's death, or the shot from one of the trailers, when she's shown in the corridor shouting to the phone (totally Misato-like shot). She's not the first in command in WILL-E, but she's totally essential, just like Misato was in the old NERV structure, filling her shoes somewhat. So, with three main female characters aligning together with each other AND against respective males, 3.0 becomes a HUGE WAR OF THE SEXES, with WILL-E being basically women of Evangelion without Rei, and NERV/SEELE being the men. I honestly don't know, where to go with this now. Hoping for interesting discussion and not a lot of nitpicking ;)
